title: Cardinal parameter models
text: Nonlinear fitting can depend starting values. helper keeps vignette robust reporting fitting problems without stopping document build.
code: safe_fit <- function(expr) {   withCallingHandlers(     tryCatch(       expr,       error = function(e) {         message("Model fit failed: ", conditionMessage(e))         NULL       }     ),     warning = function(w) {       message("Model fit warning: ", conditionMessage(w))       invokeRestart("muffleWarning")     }   ) }

title: Cardinal parameter models
text: salmonella dataset contains temperature growth-rate data Salmonella typhimurium cooked chicken.
code: data(salmonella) head(salmonella) #>   Temp    GR sqrtGR #> 1    8 0.012  0.110 #> 2   10 0.025  0.158 #> 3   12 0.048  0.219 #> 4   14 0.078  0.279 #> 5   16 0.107  0.327 #> 6   18 0.155  0.394 temp_fit <- safe_fit(   fit_cardinal(     salmonella,     model = "CMTI",     x = "Temp",     response = "sqrtGR",     start = list(Tmax = 42, Tmin = 1, MUopt = 1, Topt = 37)   ) )  temp_fit #> predmicror fit #>   type: cardinal #>   model: CMTI #>   response: sqrtGR (sqrtGR) #>   formula: sqrtGR ~ CMTI(Temp, Tmax, Tmin, MUopt, Topt) #>  #> Nonlinear regression model #>   model: sqrtGR ~ CMTI(Temp, Tmax, Tmin, MUopt, Topt) #>    data: data #>    Tmax    Tmin   MUopt    Topt  #> 49.0326  5.0268  0.7521 40.3671  #>  residual sum-of-squares: 0.005018 #>  #> Algorithm: multifit/levenberg-marquardt, (scaling: more, solver: qr) #>  #> Number of iterations to convergence: 44  #> Achieved convergence tolerance: 7.98e-17 if (!is.null(temp_fit)) {   fit_metrics(temp_fit) } #>   model     type response response_scale  n p         SSE       RMSE        MAE #> 1  CMTI cardinal   sqrtGR         sqrtGR 21 4 0.005018168 0.01545834 0.01253912 #>            bias        RSE        R2    adj_R2   logLik       AIC       BIC #> 1 -4.168898e-05 0.01718099 0.9960034 0.9950043 57.76403 -105.5281 -100.3054 #>   converged #> 1      TRUE if (!is.null(temp_fit)) {   grid <- data.frame(     Temp = seq(min(salmonella$Temp), max(salmonella$Temp), length.out = 100)   )   pred <- predmicror_augment(temp_fit, newdata = grid)    plot(     sqrtGR ~ Temp,     data = salmonella,     xlab = "Temperature",     ylab = "Square root growth rate",     pch = 19   )   if (".fitted" %in% names(pred)) {     lines(pred$Temp, pred[[".fitted"]], lwd = 2)   } }

title: Data Transformation in predmicror
text: previous versions predmicror, inconsistency required data transformation different models. models required data natural logarithm (ln) scale, others required base-10 logarithm (log10) scale. lead confusion errors. address issue, models predmicror harmonized use natural logarithm (ln) response variable Y(t). means users always provide microbial concentration ln scale.

title: Data Transformation in predmicror
text: data log10 scale, can easily convert ln scale using following formula: ln(N) = log(10) * log10(N) example convert column data frame: ensuring models use consistent ln scale, predmicror now user-friendly less prone errors.
code: # Create a sample data frame with log10 data my_data <- data.frame(   Time = c(0, 1, 2, 3),   log10N = c(2, 2.5, 3, 3.5) )  # Convert the log10N column to lnN my_data$lnN <- log(10) * my_data$log10N  # Print the updated data frame print(my_data) #>   Time log10N      lnN #> 1    0    2.0 4.605170 #> 2    1    2.5 5.756463 #> 3    2    3.0 6.907755 #> 4    3    3.5 8.059048

title: Fitting growth models using predmicror
text: start fitting Huang full growth model experimental data stored growthfull dataset. Huang full growth model function (Huang()) predmicror package function fitted data non-linear least squares function gsl_nls() implemented gslnls package. First, need good guess starting values fitting procedure: Now can fit model data growthfull: Next, can check model parameters: predict() function gslnls package can used produce confidence prediction intervals prediction lnN given Time. Plot observed data fitted values confidence interval  Plot observed data fitted values prediction interval
code: start_values <- list(Y0 = 0.02, Ymax = 22, MUmax = 1.6, lag = 5) fit <- gsl_nls(lnN ~ HuangFM(Time, Y0, Ymax, MUmax, lag),   data = growthfull,   start = start_values ) summary(fit) ##  ## Formula: lnN ~ HuangFM(Time, Y0, Ymax, MUmax, lag) ##  ## Parameters: ##       Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)     ## Y0     0.04562    0.25768   0.177    0.863     ## Ymax  21.13232    0.22393  94.372 8.54e-15 *** ## MUmax  1.85942    0.06076  30.601 2.08e-10 *** ## lag    5.07987    0.25000  20.320 7.89e-09 *** ## --- ## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 ##  ## Residual standard error: 0.4444 on 9 degrees of freedom ##  ## Number of iterations to convergence: 10  ## Achieved convergence tolerance: 2.22e-16 coef(fit) ##          Y0        Ymax       MUmax         lag  ##  0.04562408 21.13231519  1.85942431  5.07986575 newTimes <- data.frame(Time = seq(0, 24, by = 0.1)) fits <- data.frame(predict(fit, newdata = newTimes, interval = "confidence", level = 0.95)) str(fits) ## 'data.frame':    241 obs. of  3 variables: ##  $ fit: num  0.0456 0.0456 0.0456 0.0456 0.0456 ... ##  $ lwr: num  -0.537 -0.537 -0.537 -0.537 -0.537 ... ##  $ upr: num  0.629 0.629 0.629 0.629 0.629 ... preds <- data.frame(predict(fit, newdata = newTimes, interval = "prediction", level = 0.95)) str(preds) ## 'data.frame':    241 obs. of  3 variables: ##  $ fit: num  0.0456 0.0456 0.0456 0.0456 0.0456 ... ##  $ lwr: num  -1.12 -1.12 -1.12 -1.12 -1.12 ... ##  $ upr: num  1.21 1.21 1.21 1.21 1.21 ... plot(newTimes$Time, fits$fit,   type = "l", col = "blue",   xlab = "time (days)", ylab = "logN",   main = "Huang full model" ) points(growthfull$Time, growthfull$lnN) lines(newTimes$Time, fits$upr, col = "red") lines(newTimes$Time, fits$lwr, col = "red") plot(newTimes$Time, fits$fit,   type = "l", col = "blue",   xlab = "time (days)", ylab = "logN", ylim = c(-1, 22), xlim = c(0, 24),   main = "Huang full model" ) points(growthfull$Time, growthfull$lnN) lines(newTimes$Time, preds$upr, col = "red") lines(newTimes$Time, preds$lwr, col = "red")

text: First, want load data set: LetÔÇÖs start Fang model, fit model experimental data using nonlinear least squares function gsl_nls() implemented gsl_nls R package: Next, can check model parameters: Next, can extract model parameters apply model new data Plot observed data fitted values confidence interval  Plot observed data fitted values prediction interval

Error t value Pr(>|t|)     ## Y0     1.75827    0.36368   4.835  0.00189 **  ## Ymax  21.13234    0.24673  85.650 7.79e-12 *** ## MUmax  1.85922    0.06619  28.091 1.86e-08 *** ## --- ## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 ##  ## Residual standard error: 0.4896 on 7 degrees of freedom ##  ## Number of iterations to convergence: 9  ## Achieved convergence tolerance: 1.776e-15 newTimes <- data.frame(Time = seq(0, 18, by = 0.1)) fits <- data.frame(predict(fit, newdata = newTimes, interval = "confidence", level = 0.95)) str(fits) ## 'data.frame':    181 obs. of  3 variables: ##  $ fit: num  1.76 1.94 2.13 2.32 2.5 ... ##  $ lwr: num  0.898 1.097 1.295 1.494 1.692 ... ##  $ upr: num  2.62 2.79 2.96 3.14 3.31 ... preds <- data.frame(predict(fit, newdata = newTimes, interval = "prediction", level = 0.95)) plot(newTimes$Time, fits$fit,   type = "l", col = "blue",   xlab = "time (days)", ylab = "logN",   main = "Fang no lag model" ) points(growthnolag$Time, growthnolag$lnN) lines(newTimes$Time, fits$upr, col = "red") lines(newTimes$Time, fits$lwr, col = "red") plot(newTimes$Time, fits$fit,   type = "l", col = "blue",   xlab = "time (days)", ylab = "logN", ylim = c(-1, 22), xlim = c(0, 18),   main = "Fang no lag model" ) points(growthnolag$Time, growthnolag$lnN) lines(newTimes$Time, preds$upr, col = "red") lines(newTimes$Time, preds$lwr, col = "red")

title: Microbial inactivation models
text: Nonlinear fitting can sensitive starting values. helper keeps vignette robust across platforms returning NULL candidate model fitted selected starting values. simple starting point fit Weibull-Mafart model. Additional candidate models can fitted compared converge.
code: safe_fit <- function(expr) {   withCallingHandlers(     tryCatch(       expr,       error = function(e) {         message("Model fit failed: ", conditionMessage(e))         NULL       }     ),     warning = function(w) {       message("Model fit warning: ", conditionMessage(w))       invokeRestart("muffleWarning")     }   ) } weibull <- safe_fit(   fit_inactivation(     mafart2005Li11,     model = "WeibullM",     time = "Time",     response = "logN",     start = list(Y0 = max(mafart2005Li11$logN), sigma = 3, alpha = 1)   ) )  weibull #> predmicror fit #>   type: inactivation #>   model: WeibullM #>   response: logN (log10N) #>   formula: logN ~ WeibullM(Time, Y0, sigma, alpha) #>  #> Nonlinear regression model #>   model: logN ~ WeibullM(Time, Y0, sigma, alpha) #>    data: data #>      Y0   sigma   alpha  #> 10.6394 13.1942  0.7727  #>  residual sum-of-squares: 1.116 #>  #> Algorithm: multifit/levenberg-marquardt, (scaling: more, solver: qr) #>  #> Number of iterations to convergence: 16  #> Achieved convergence tolerance: 1.11e-15 weibull_ph <- safe_fit(   fit_inactivation(     mafart2005Li11,     model = "WeibullPH",     time = "Time",     response = "logN",     start = list(Y0 = max(mafart2005Li11$logN), k = 0.3, alpha = 1)   ) )  huang_rgs <- safe_fit(   fit_inactivation(     mafart2005Li11,     model = "HuangRGS",     time = "Time",     response = "logN",     start = list(Y0 = max(mafart2005Li11$logN), k = 0.3, M = 1)   ) ) #> Model fit warning: singular gradient matrix at parameter estimates  fits <- Filter(Negate(is.null), list(   WeibullM = weibull,   WeibullPH = weibull_ph,   HuangRGS = huang_rgs ))  names(fits) #> [1] "WeibullM"  "WeibullPH" "HuangRGS"

title: Introduction to predmicror

title: Introduction to predmicror
text: actual version includes primary growth models describe microbial concentration function time constant environmental conditions. model inputs : tt: time, assuming time zero beginning experiment; Y(t)Y_{(t)}: natural logarithm microbial concentration X(t)X_{(t)} measured time tt. Users make sure microbial concentration input entered natural logarithm, Y(t)=ln(X(t))Y_{(t)} = ln(X_{(t)}). number model parameters dependent upon completeness microbial growth curve. following parameters can estimated using web application: Y0Y_0: natural logarithm initial microbial concentration t=0t=0; ╬╝max\mu_{max}: maximum specific growth rate given time unitsÔłĺ1units^{-1}; ╬╗\lambda: duration lag phase time units; YmaxY_{max}: natural logarithm maximum concentration reached microorganism. full model adjusted complete microbial curve, lag phase, exponential phase stationary phase can identified. predmicror can also fit reduced models. -stationary phase model adjusted experimental microbial curve presents lag phase exponential phase, whereas -lag phase model adjusted experimental curve composed exponential phase stationary phase. experimental growth curve presents exponential phase analysed using predmicror functions.

title: Summary
text: Predictive microbiology uses mathematical models describe microbial populations grow decline controlled conditions. predmicror package provides R interface fitting widely used predictive microbiology models experimental data, supporting research food safety, shelf-life estimation, process design. includes primary growth models (e.g., Huang, Baranyi, reparameterized Gompertz) [@Huang2008; @Baranyi1993Modelling; @Zwietering1990], inactivation models (e.g., Weibull-type survival curves) [@Boekel2002], cardinal models describe effects temperature, pH, water activity, inhibitors growth rates [@Rosso1995]. package standardizes response variables natural log scale ships example datasets vignettes, enabling reproducible model fitting visualization. optional local assistant (CLI Shiny app) helps users select models draft gsl_nls workflows parameter estimation.

title: Summary
text: Predictive microbiology models cornerstone quantitative microbial risk assessment food process validation, researchers often re-implement model equations fitting workflows ad hoc scripts. practice makes results difficult reproduce compare across studies. tools GInaFiT provide accessible inactivation model fitting spreadsheet workflows [@Geeraerd2005], limited scope integrate well R-based analysis pipelines. predmicror addresses gap offering single, consistent R API growth, inactivation, cardinal models drawn canonical literature [@Baranyi1993Modelling; @Huang2008; @Zwietering1990; @Boekel2002; @Rosso1995]. package designed used directly nonlinear regression procedures (via gsl_nls), supports transparent reproducible parameter estimation workflows [@Dolan2013]. Included datasets vignettes provide reference analyses common experimental designs, optional assistant lowers entry barrier new users translating natural language questions runnable R code. predmicror suitable classroom demonstrations, exploratory analysis, reproducible research pipelines. built-vignettes datasets illustrate full workflows growth, inactivation, cardinal modeling across common conditions (e.g., temperature, pH, water activity), provide ready--adapt templates new studies.

code: library(gslnls) data(growthfull) initial_values <- list(Y0 = -0.1, Ymax = 22, MUmax = 1.7, lag = 5) fit <- gsl_nls(lnN ~ BaranyiFM(Time, Y0, Ymax, MUmax, lag),   data = growthfull,   start = initial_values ) summary(fit) #>  #> Formula: lnN ~ BaranyiFM(Time, Y0, Ymax, MUmax, lag) #>  #> Parameters: #>       Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)     #> Y0     2.403e+00  1.829e-02 131.399  < 2e-16 *** #> Yres  -6.220e+00  4.897e+06   0.000        1     #> sigma  1.287e+01  6.828e-01  18.853 2.39e-11 *** #> alpha  2.395e+00  2.720e-01   8.807 4.40e-07 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ÔÇś***ÔÇÖ 0.001 ÔÇś**ÔÇÖ 0.01 ÔÇś*ÔÇÖ 0.05 ÔÇś.ÔÇÖ 0.1 ÔÇś ÔÇÖ 1 #>  #> Residual standard error: 0.04348 on 14 degrees of freedom #>  #> Number of iterations to convergence: 38  #> Achieved convergence tolerance: 1.159e-11 #>   plot(logN ~ Time, data = bixina) lines(bixina$Time, predict(fit), col = "blue")

title: predmicror 1.3.0
text: Added applied vignette microbial inactivation models using fit_inactivation(), predmicror_augment(), fit_metrics(), compare_models(). Added applied vignette cardinal parameter models using fit_cardinal(), diagnostics helpers, model comparison tools. Expanded examples showing safer post-fitting workflows prediction new data grids.

title: predmicror 1.2.1
text: Register default S3 methods predmicror_augment() fit_metrics() roxygen documentation. Add pkgdown site URL DESCRIPTION. Add new fitting diagnostic topics pkgdown reference index. Ignore temporary phase overlay folders created applying local hotfixes.

title: predmicror 1.2.0
text: Add predmicror_augment() extract original data, fitted values, residuals, model name, model type predmicror_fit objects. Add .data.frame.predmicror_fit() lightweight base-R shortcut predmicror_augment(). Add fit_metrics() calculate residual diagnostics information criteria fitted models, including SSE, RMSE, MAE, bias, residual standard error, R2, adjusted R2, log-likelihood, AIC, BIC, convergence status. Add compare_models() combine diagnostics across multiple fitted models sort AIC, BIC, RMSE, MAE. Add tests diagnostic extraction, model metrics, model comparison. Add model-comparison vignette showing compare alternative fitted predictive microbiology models.
